findings.message: Removing the fallback to `comment?.member?.name` means that when `member` is null or has no name, `memberName` will be undefined instead of falling back to the parent comment author's name. This could cause the form to display no name or break UI logic that expects a string.
findings.suggestion: If the intent is to never inherit the parent's name, ensure the UI handles an undefined `memberName` gracefully (e.g., show a placeholder like 'Anonymous'). If the fallback is still needed for some cases, consider a conditional fallback based on context.

findings.message: Changing `in_reply_to_id` from `openForm.in_reply_to_id` to `parent.id` may alter the reply threading logic. If `openForm.in_reply_to_id` could differ from `parent.id` (e.g., when replying to a nested reply), this change could break the intended reply chain.
findings.suggestion: Verify that `parent.id` is always the correct target for the reply. If the form can be opened for a nested reply, `openForm.in_reply_to_id` might be more accurate.

lines.content: // Disable editing if the member doesn't have a name or when we are submitting the form
openStyles = isReplyToReply ? 'pl-[1px] pt-[68px] sm:pl-[44px] sm:pt-[56px]' : 'pl-[1px] pt-[48px] sm:pl-[44px] sm:pt-[40px]';
test('Reply form does not inherit expertise from parent comment author', async ({page}) => {
// Set logged-in member with NO expertise
// Add a comment from a different member who HAS expertise

html: '

This is a comment from someone with expertise

',
member: buildMember({name: 'Expert User', expertise: 'Head of Comments'})
// Verify the parent comment shows the author's expertise
// Click reply on the parent comment
// Wait for reply form to appear
// The reply form should show the logged-in member's info, NOT the parent's
// The expertise button should show "Add your expertise" since the
// logged-in member has no expertise - it should NOT show "Head of Comments"
test('Reply form does not inherit name from parent comment author', async ({page}) => {
// Set logged-in member with NO name (null triggers the fallback bug)
// Add a comment from a different member who HAS a name

html: '

This is a comment from someone with a name

',
// Verify the parent comment shows the author's name
// Click reply on the parent comment
// Wait for reply form to appear
// The reply form should NOT show the parent comment author's name
// It should either show nothing, "Anonymous", or prompt for a name

Added line: <FormWrapper editor={editor} isOpen={true} openForm={openForm} reduced={isMobile()}>

entity:apps/comments-ui/test/e2e/actions.message: Changed module apps.comments-ui.test.e2e.actions.test touched by 1 hunk(s).

keep.message: Removing the fallback to `comment?.member?.name` means that when `member` is null or has no name, `memberName` will be undefined instead of falling back to the parent comment author's name. This could cause the form to display no name or break UI logic that expects a string.
keep.suggestion: If the intent is to never inherit the parent's name, ensure the UI handles an undefined `memberName` gracefully (e.g., show a placeholder like 'Anonymous'). If the fallback is still needed for some cases, consider a conditional fallback based on context.

keep.message: Changing `in_reply_to_id` from `openForm.in_reply_to_id` to `parent.id` may alter the reply threading logic. If `openForm.in_reply_to_id` could differ from `parent.id` (e.g., when replying to a nested reply), this change could break the intended reply chain.
keep.suggestion: Verify that `parent.id` is always the correct target for the reply. If the form can be opened for a nested reply, `openForm.in_reply_to_id` might be more accurate.
